query: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repair status latest news June July 2026, algae paint peeling, is the pool fully repaired, lawsuit status
answer:
Research complete. Here's the summary of findings on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repair status:

## Bottom Line (as of July 1, 2026)
The pool is **NOT fully repaired**. Despite White House claims it is "fixed, crystal clear," the coating is still peeling and algae has been a recurring problem.

## Key Findings

**Repair status & timeline:**
- NPS confirmed in a **June 25, 2026 federal court filing** (declaration by Deputy Director Frank Lands) that it will **drain the pool again AFTER July 4, 2026** to repair lining damage. The pool stays filled through Independence Day.
- Trump announced June 21 that repairs would begin "immediately" and June 23 that it would be drained around July 4 for "permanent repair."
- **No firm public completion date** for the post-July-4 repair phase exists. Earlier estimates referenced "weeks"; experts say a proper fix requires a "failure analysis" (implying longer).

**Lawsuit status:**
- The **Cultural Landscape Foundation's lawsuit to halt the work is PENDING** in U.S. District Court for D.C. (docket dcd.292242). It alleges the project bypassed Congressionally-mandated historic-preservation review. The June 25 NPS filing was the government's response; **no ruling halting the work has been reported**.

**Physical condition:**
- Peeling: ongoing (Rhino Pipeliner 5000/RHINO products; experts blame rushed application and lack of flexibility over expansion joints).
- Algae: recurring; nanobubbler ozone tech reportedly cleared the water by late June.

**Costs:** ~$16–16.4M total (~8x the original ~$1.8M estimate) — $14.65M to Atlantic Industrial Coatings + $1.74M to Green Water Solutions, both no-bid.

**Disputed cause:** Administration blames vandals (14 cited as of June 30, per US Attorney Pirro); NYT/experts blame a rushed job, removal of nanobubblers for a UFC event, and the presidential motorcade driving over the drained pool. A new July 1 environmental controversy involves dead ducks and the coating's aquatic toxicity.

Full structured findings with all sources and data points have been submitted.

sources.title: Park Service will drain, repair Reflecting Pool after July Fourth, court filing says
sources.snippet: NPS plans to drain and repair the pool after July 4th per Frank Lands court filing; details algae, peeling, vandalism claims, and the nonprofit lawsuit.

sources.title: Reflecting Pool liner was cut with a sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
sources.snippet: $16M project; Cultural Landscape Foundation lawsuit; NPS to drain after Independence Day; no-bid contracts to Green Water Solutions ($1.7M) and Atlantic Industrial Coatings ($14.7M).

sources.title: Reflecting Pool Won't Be Drained Until After July 4, New Filing Shows
sources.snippet: Frank Lands' court declaration says the pool will not be drained until after July 4, 2026 to repair lining damage.

sources.title: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool Is Being Drained -- Again
sources.snippet: NPS to drain after July 4; peeling after hydrogen peroxide algae treatment; $14M project originally estimated $1.5-2M; Cultural Landscape Foundation lawsuit re Congressionally-mandated consultation.

sources.title: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or blade, National Park Service says
sources.snippet: $16M rehabilitation; Cultural Landscape Foundation seeking to halt work; algae bloom and peeling coating; NPS to begin draining after Independence Day.

sources.title: Timeline: Trump's 2026 Reflecting Pool renovation project
sources.snippet: Timeline: April announcement ($1.5-2M, 1-2 weeks); June algae bloom; nanobubble second contractor; total ~$16M; May 2026 lawsuit by nonprofit.

sources.title: Reflecting Pool renovations to cost more than $16 million
sources.snippet: Repainting cost $14.65M (over $4M above original), plus $1.74M to Green Water Solutions for nanobubble system; algae and peeling since repainting.

sources.title: Trump says Reflecting Pool repairs will begin 'immediately' after vandalism arrests
sources.snippet: June 21 Trump statement work begins immediately; five arrested; Atlantic Industrial Coatings said drainage needed and covered under warranty.

sources.title: 'Permanent repair' to Reflecting Pool to come around July 4, Trump says
sources.snippet: June 23 Trump announced pool will be drained again for 'permanent repair' around July 4; >$16M cost; algae and peeling.

sources.title: How the Reflecting Pool turned green: Missing 'bubblers' and a rush job
sources.snippet: Nanobubblers removed for June 12 UFC event caused algae within 36 hours; Rhino Pipeliner 5000 peeling due to lack of flexibility; White House says pool fixed while Park Service plans to drain again.

sources.title: Firm Behind 2010 Reflecting Pool Fix Rebuffed Offer for This One
sources.snippet: Sika Corp rejected March 2026 offer citing 2.5 miles of expansion joints as 'unfeasible'; Atlantic Industrial Coatings used Rhino Linings products; cost surged from ~$1.8M to ~$15M; motorcade drove over drained pool in May.

sources.title: Analysis: Lincoln Reflecting Pool accounts for nearly entire spike in NPS urgent no-bid contracts
sources.snippet: Center for Western Priorities: $16.4M (77%) of $21.2M NPS urgent no-bid contracts went to two Reflecting Pool contracts; NYT documents show staff aware of expansion joint cuts a week before peeling.

sources.title: US Attorney Jeanine Pirro Says 14 Cited for Reflecting Pool Vandalism
sources.snippet: June 30: Pirro says 14 cited, up to 10 years; NPS plans to drain after July 4; Greenwater spokeswoman says clear water from ozone nanobubble technology.

sources.title: Ohio Firm Clears Reflecting Pool Amid Scrutiny
sources.snippet: Greenwater Services $1.7M contract for NanoBubble Ozone Technology; water turned clear late June; part-owner John J. Cafaro is a Trump friend; NPS plans to drain after Independence Day.

sources.title: Trump Reflecting Pool contractor used toxic liner with 'long lasting effects': report
sources.snippet: July 1 report: RHINO 405 epoxy labeled toxic to aquatic life; dead ducks reported; Center for Biological Diversity calls for investigation.

sources.title: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
sources.snippet: In 2026 the pool was resurfaced at Trump's order with a blue polyurea coating and a nanobubbler filtration system was added; pool is 2,030-by-167 feet.

data_points.metric: Green Water Solutions/Greenwater Services nanobubbler algae contract (no-bid)

Fence post tops thrown into pool (per NPS filing)
